channel.title: IT-Security & Privacy News Aggregator
channel.description: Aggregierter RSS-Feed aus führenden IT-Security, Privacy und Cyber Policy Quellen

item.title: Microsoft Bets $10 Billion to Boost Japan's AI, Cybersecurity
item.description: The deal aims to accelerate AI adoption, train workers, and develop cybersecurity partnerships — the latest move by a hyperscaler to compete for sovereign AI and data centers.
item.dc:creator: Robert Lemos

item.title: Patch Tuesday, April 2026 Edition
item.description: Microsoft today pushed software updates to fix a staggering 167 security vulnerabilities in its Windows operating systems and related software, including a SharePoint Server zero-day and a publicly disclosed weakness in Windows Defender dubbed "BlueHammer." Separately, Google Chrome fixed its fourth zero-day of 2026, and an emergency update for Adobe Reader nixes an actively exploited flaw that can lead to remote code execution.
item.dc:creator: BrianKrebs

item.title: Privilege Elevation Dominates Massive Microsoft Patch Update
item.description: Elevation-of-privilege bugs accounted for more than half of the 165 vulnerabilities patched, with two zero-days in that mix.
item.dc:creator: Jai Vijayan

item.title: EDR-Killer Ecosystem Expansion Requires Stronger BYOVD Defenses
item.description: Stopping EDR killers, which employ bring-your-own-vulnerable-driver (BYOVD) attack techniques, is difficult, but not impossible.
item.dc:creator: Rob Wright

item.title: Adobe Issues Emergency Patch for Critical PDF Flaw Exploited For Months
item.description: <p>Adobe patches a critical PDF flaw exploited for months, allowing attackers to bypass sandbox protections and deliver malware. Users urged to update now.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-adobe-critical-pdf-flaw-exploited-months-emergency-patch/">Adobe Issues Emergency Patch for Critical PDF Flaw Exploited For Months</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Joseph Ofonagoro

item.title: Spotlight On: Stripe, a New Principal Participating Organization
item.dc:creator: amalone@pcisecuritystandards.org (Alicia Malone)

item.title: War Game Exercise Demonstrates How Social Media Manipulation Works
item.description: In an educational game called &quot;Capture the Narrative,&quot; students created bots to sway a fictional election, simulating influence in real-world political scenarios.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Montalbano

item.title: Upcoming Speaking Engagements
item.dc:creator: Bruce Schneier

item.title: New PHP Composer Flaws Enable Arbitrary Command Execution — Patches Released
item.description:
Two high-severity security vulnerabilities&nbsp;have been disclosed in Composer, a package&nbsp;manager for&nbsp;PHP, that, if successfully exploited, could result in arbitrary command execution. The vulnerabilities&nbsp;have been&nbsp;described as command injection flaws affecting the Perforce VCS (version control software) driver. Details of the two flaws are below&nbsp;-
CVE-2026-40176 (CVSS
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Google Adds Rust-Based DNS Parser into Pixel 10 Modem to Enhance Security
item.description: Google has announced the integration of a Rust-based Domain Name System (DNS) parser into the modem firmware as part of its ongoing efforts to beef up the security of Pixel devices and push memory-safe code at a more foundational&nbsp;level. "The new Rust-based DNS parser significantly reduces our security risk by mitigating an entire class of vulnerabilities in a risky area, while also laying
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: AI-Driven Pushpaganda Scam Exploits Google Discover to Spread Scareware and Ad Fraud
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have unmasked a novel ad fraud scheme that has been found to leverage search engine poisoning (SEO) techniques and artificial intelligence (AI)-generated content to push deceptive news stories into&nbsp;Google's Discover&nbsp;feed and trick users into enabling persistent browser notifications that lead to scareware and financial&nbsp;scams. The campaign, which has been
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Google $135M Settlement: Millions of Android Users May Qualify for Payout
item.description: <p>Android users may be eligible for a payout from Google’s $135 million settlement over background data use. Here’s who qualifies and how to claim.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-google-135m-android-data-settlement-payout/">Google $135M Settlement: Millions of Android Users May Qualify for Payout</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Aminu Abdullahi

item.title: CISOs Urged to Innovate with Talent Retention as Job Satisfaction Declines
item.description: A new IANS report claims just 34% of cybersecurity professionals plan to stay put in the next 12 months

item.title: Triad Nexus Expands Global Fraud Operations Despite US Sanctions
item.description: Triad Nexus scales $200m scams, uses infrastructure laundering, localized fraud and US-access blocks

item.title: Malicious Chrome Extensions Campaign Exposes User Data
item.description: 108 malicious Chrome extensions steal sessions, Google data, inject ads via single C2 infrastructure

item.title: How Hackers Are Thinking About AI
item.description: <p>Interesting paper: &#8220;<a href="https://arxiv.org/abs/2602.14783">What hackers talk about when they talk about AI: Early-stage diffusion of a cybercrime innovation.</a>&#8221;</p> <blockquote><p><b>Abstract:</b> The rapid expansion of artificial intelligence (AI) is raising concerns about its potential to transform cybercrime. Beyond empowering novice offenders, AI stands to intensify the scale and sophistication of attacks by seasoned cybercriminals. This paper examines the evolving relationship between cybercriminals and AI using a unique dataset from a cyber threat intelligence platform. Analyzing more than 160 cybercrime forum conversations collected over seven months, our research reveals how cybercriminals understand AI and discuss how they can exploit its capabilities. Their exchanges reflect growing curiosity about AI&#8217;s criminal applications through legal tools and dedicated criminal tools, but also doubts and anxieties about AI&#8217;s effectiveness and its effects on their business models and operational security. The study documents attempts to misuse legitimate AI tools and develop bespoke models tailored for illicit purposes. Combining the diffusion of innovation framework with thematic analysis, the paper provides an in-depth view of emerging AI-enabled cybercrime and offers practical insights for law enforcement and policymakers...</p></blockquote>
item.dc:creator: Bruce Schneier

item.title: Mirax Android RAT Turns Devices into SOCKS5 Proxies, Reaching 220,000 via Meta Ads
item.description: A nascent Android remote access trojan&nbsp;called Mirax has been observed actively targeting Spanish-speaking countries, with campaigns reaching more than 220,000 accounts on Facebook, Instagram, Messenger, and Threads through advertisements on&nbsp;Meta. "Mirax integrates advanced Remote Access Trojan (RAT) capabilities, allowing threat actors to fully interact with compromised devices in real
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Analysis of 216M Security Findings Shows a 4x Increase In Critical Risk (2026 Report)
item.description: OX Security recently analyzed 216 million security findings across 250 organizations over a 90-day period. The&nbsp;primary takeaway: while raw alert volume grew by 52% year-over-year, prioritized critical risk grew by nearly&nbsp;400%. The surge in AI-assisted development is creating a "velocity&nbsp;gap" where the density of high-impact vulnerabilities&nbsp;is scaling faster&nbsp;than
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: AI Security Institute Advocates Security Best Practices After Mythos Test
item.description: The AISI has issued its judgement on Anthropic’s Mythos Preview model

item.title: FIFA World Cup 2026: More than One-Third of Official Partners Expose the Public to the Risk of Email Fraud
108 Malicious Chrome Extensions Steal Google and Telegram Data, Affecting 20,000 Users
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have discovered a new campaign in which a cluster of 108 Google Chrome extensions has been found to communicate with the same command-and-control (C2) infrastructure with the goal of collecting user data and enabling browser-level abuse by injecting ads and arbitrary JavaScript code into every web page&nbsp;visited. According to Socket, the extensions (complete list
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: ShowDoc RCE Flaw CVE-2025-0520 Actively Exploited on Unpatched Servers
item.description: A critical security vulnerability&nbsp;impacting ShowDoc, a document management and collaboration service popular in China, has come under active exploitation in the&nbsp;wild. The vulnerability in question&nbsp;is CVE-2025-0520 (aka CNVD-2020-26585), which carries a CVSS score of 9.4&nbsp;out of&nbsp;10.0. It relates to a case of unrestricted file upload that stems from improper validation of
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: CISA Adds 6 Known Exploited Flaws in Fortinet, Microsoft, and Adobe Software
item.description:
The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) on&nbsp;Monday added half a dozen security flaws to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities&nbsp;(KEV) catalog, citing evidence of active exploitation. The list of vulnerabilities is as follows&nbsp;-
CVE-2026-21643 (CVSS score: 9.1) -&nbsp; An SQL injection vulnerability in&nbsp; Fortinet FortiClient EMS that could allow an
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Why Orgs Need to Test Networks to Withstand DDoS Attacks During Peak Loads
item.description: Security teams can't test distributed denial-of-service defenses in a vacuum. They need to test during periods of high demand, such as tax filing deadlines.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Andriani

item.title: CSA: CISOs Should Prepare for Post-Mythos Exploit Storm
item.description: In a new report from the Cloud Security Alliance (CSA), experts warn of an &quot;AI vulnerability storm&quot; triggered by the introduction of Anthropic's Claude Mythos.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Culafi

item.title: Adobe Patches Actively Exploited Zero-Day That Lingered for Months
item.description: An attacker has been using maliciously crafted PDF files to exploit a zero-day in Adobe Acrobat and Reader for at least four months.
item.dc:creator: Jai Vijayan

item.title: GrafanaGhost: The AI That Leaked Everything Without Being Hacked
item.description: <p>A newly disclosed vulnerability reveals how AI assistants can become invisible channels for data exfiltration — and why security enforcement must shift to the data layer.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-grafanaghost-ai-data-exfiltration-security-risk/">GrafanaGhost: The AI That Leaked Everything Without Being Hacked</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Tim Freestone

item.title: Empty Attestations: OT Lacks the Tools for Cryptographic Readiness
item.description: OT asset owners are being asked by regulators to attest to their post-quantum cryptographic readiness without the appropriate tooling, resulting in paperwork dressed up to look like genuine security.
item.dc:creator: Brad McInnis

item.title: Booking.com Hack Exposes Customer Data, Sparks Travel Scam Fears
item.description: <p>Booking.com confirms a data breach that exposed traveler details, raising urgent concerns about highly targeted phishing scams and customer safety.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-booking-com-data-breach-traveler-scam-risk/">Booking.com Hack Exposes Customer Data, Sparks Travel Scam Fears</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Aminu Abdullahi

item.title: JanelaRAT Malware Targets Latin American Banks with 14,739 Attacks in Brazil in 2025
item.description: Banks and financial institutions in Latin American countries like Brazil and Mexico have continued to be the target of a malware family called JanelaRAT. A modified version of BX RAT, JanelaRAT is known to steal financial and cryptocurrency data associated with specific financial entities, as well as track mouse inputs, log keystrokes, take screenshots, and collect system metadata. "One of the
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: On Anthropic’s Mythos Preview and Project Glasswing
item.description: <p>The cybersecurity industry is obsessing over Anthropic&#8217;s new model, Claude Mythos Preview, and its effects on cybersecurity. Anthropic said that it is <a href="https://red.anthropic.com/2026/mythos-preview/">not releasing it</a> to the general public because of its cyberattack capabilities, and has launched <a href="https://www.anthropic.com/glasswing">Project Glasswing</a> to run the model against a whole slew of public domain and proprietary software, with the aim of finding and patching all the vulnerabilities before hackers get their hands on the model and exploit them.</p> <p>There&#8217;s a lot here, and I hope to write something more considered in the coming week, but I want to make some quick observations...</p>
item.dc:creator: Bruce Schneier

item.title: APT41 Delivers 'Zero-Detection' Backdoor to Harvest Cloud Credentials
item.description: The prolific China-backed threat group is targeting AWS, Google, Azure, and Alibaba cloud environments and using typosquatting to obscure C2 communication.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Montalbano

item.title: Mailbox Rule Abuse Emerges as Stealthy Post-Compromise Threat
item.description: Attackers are abusing Microsoft 365 mailbox rules to hide activity, exfiltrate data and retain access after account compromise, researchers warn

item.title: FBI and Indonesian Police Dismantle W3LL Phishing Network Behind $20M Fraud Attempts
item.description: The U.S. Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI), in partnership with the Indonesian National Police, has dismantled the infrastructure associated with a global phishing operation that leveraged an off-the-shelf toolkit&nbsp;called W3LL to steal thousands of victims' account credentials and attempt more than $20 million in&nbsp;fraud. In tandem, authorities detained the alleged developer, who has&
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Mirax Android Trojan Turns Devices Into Residential Proxy Nodes
item.description: Security researchers warn of Mirax, an emerging Android banking trojan using MaaS, remote access and residential proxies to target European users

item.title: ⚡ Weekly Recap: Fiber Optic Spying, Windows Rootkit, AI Vulnerability Hunting and More
item.description: Monday&nbsp;is back, and the weekend’s backlog of chaos is officially hitting the fan. We&nbsp;are tracking a critical zero-day that has been quietly living in your PDFs for months, plus some aggressive state-sponsored meddling in infrastructure that is finally coming to light. It&nbsp;is one of those mornings where the gap between a quiet shift and a full-blown incident response is basically
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Your MTTD Looks Great. Your Post-Alert Gap Doesn't
item.description: Anthropic restricted its Mythos Preview model last week after it autonomously found and exploited zero-day vulnerabilities in every major operating system and browser. Palo&nbsp;Alto Networks' Wendi&nbsp;Whitmore warned that similar capabilities are weeks or months from proliferation. CrowdStrike's 2026 Global Threat Report puts average eCrime breakout time at 29 minutes. Mandiant's M-Trends
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: FBI Dismantles $20m Phishing Operation W3LL
item.description: The W3LL phishing kit has been associated with fraud attempts totaling $20m

item.title: AI Chatbots and Trust
item.dc:creator: Bruce Schneier

item.title: North Korea's APT37 Uses Facebook Social Engineering to Deliver RokRAT Malware
item.description: The North Korean hacking group tracked&nbsp;as APT37 (aka ScarCruft) has been attributed to a fresh multi-stage, social engineering campaign in which threat actors approached targets on Facebook and added them as friends on the social media platform, turning the trust-building exercise into a delivery channel for a remote access trojan&nbsp;called RokRAT. "The threat actor used two Facebook
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: UK Cyber Security Council Launches Associate Cyber Security Professional Title
item.description: The UK Cyber Security Council has unveiled a new Associate Cyber Security Professional title aimed at supporting early‑career cybersecurity professionals

item.title: Operation Atlantic Seizes $12m in Crypto Losses
item.description: UK, US and Canadian authorities have identified over 20,000 victims of approval phishing scams that trick users into handing over full crypto wallet access

item.title: Cyber TPRM vs Compliance TPRM: Which is right for you? | UpGuard
item.description: Cyber TPRM and compliance TPRM answer different questions and serve different buyers. Learn how to tell them apart and decide which is best for you.

item.title: OpenAI Revokes macOS App Certificate After Malicious Axios Supply Chain Incident
item.description: OpenAI revealed a GitHub Actions workflow used to sign its macOS apps led to the download of the malicious Axios library on March 31, but noted that no user data or internal system was compromised. "Out of an abundance of caution, we are taking steps to protect the process that certifies our macOS applications are legitimate OpenAI apps,"&nbsp;OpenAI said in a post last week. "We found no
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: CPUID Breach Distributes STX RAT via Trojanized CPU-Z and HWMonitor Downloads
item.description: Unknown threat actors compromised CPUID ("cpuid[.]com"), a website&nbsp;that hosts popular hardware monitoring tools like CPU-Z, HWMonitor, HWMonitor Pro, and PerfMonitor, for less than 24 hours to serve malicious executables for the software and deploy a remote access trojan called STX&nbsp;RAT. The incident lasted from approximately April 9, 15:00 UTC, to about April 10, 10:00&nbsp;UTC, with
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Adobe Patches Actively Exploited Acrobat Reader Flaw CVE-2026-34621
item.description: Adobe has released emergency updates to fix a critical security flaw in Acrobat Reader that&nbsp;has come under active exploitation in the&nbsp;wild. The vulnerability, assigned the CVE identifier CVE-2026-34621, carries a CVSS score of 8.6 out of 10.0. Successful exploitation of the flaw could allow an attacker to run malicious code on affected installations. It&nbsp;has been&nbsp;described as
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Citizen Lab: Law Enforcement Used Webloc to Track 500 Million Devices via Ad Data
item.description: Hungarian domestic intelligence, the national police in El Salvador, and several U.S. law&nbsp;enforcement and police departments have been attributed to the use of an advertising-based global geolocation surveillance system called&nbsp;Webloc. The tool was developed by Israeli company Cobwebs Technologies and is now sold by its successor Penlink after&nbsp;the two firms merged in July&nbsp;2023
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Friday Squid Blogging: Squid Overfishing in the South Pacific
item.dc:creator: Bruce Schneier

item.title: Hims Breach Exposes the Most Sensitive Kinds of PHI
item.description: Threat actors breached the telehealth brand, and now they may know patients' personal health details. What could they do with that information?
item.dc:creator: Nate Nelson

item.title: Your Next Breach Will Look Like Business as Usual
item.description: These are the fundamental detection model shifts cybersecurity teams need to make to keep up with the rising number of credential-based attacks.
item.dc:creator: Jeanette Miller-Osborn

item.title: SMB Compliance & Protection Without the Guesswork
item.description: <p>As someone who has worked closely with small and mid-sized businesses, I see the same challenge come up again and again: you know compliance and security matter, but actually getting there feels overwhelming, expensive, and unclear. Maybe you&#8217;ve looked into it before and hit a wall of complex frameworks, expensive consultants, or generic advice that ...</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/resource-library/feature/smb-compliance-protection-bundle/">SMB Compliance &amp; Protection Without the Guesswork</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>

item.title: Microsoft: Third-Party Android Vulnerability Leaves Over 50M Users Exposed
item.description: <p>A flaw in the EngageLab SDK exposed 50 million Android users, allowing malicious apps to exploit trusted permissions and access sensitive data.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-engagelab-sdk-android-vulnerability-malware-bridge/">Microsoft: Third-Party Android Vulnerability Leaves Over 50M Users Exposed</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Joseph Ofonagoro

item.title: FINRA Launches Financial Intelligence Fusion Center to Combat Cybersecurity and Fraud Threats
Orange Business Reimagines Enterprise Voice Communications With Trust and AI
AI Expansion, Security Crises, and Workforce Upheaval Define This Week in Tech
item.description: <p>See what you missed in Daily Tech Insider from April 6–10.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/ai-expansion-security-crises-and-workforce-upheaval-define-this-week-in-tech/">AI Expansion, Security Crises, and Workforce Upheaval Define This Week in Tech</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: TechRepublic Staff

item.title: Emerging Risks: Typosquatting in the MCP Ecosystem | UpGuard
item.description: The MCP server ecosystem is susceptible to brand impersonation for both local and remote servers.

item.title: Industrial Controllers Still Vulnerable As Conflicts Move to Cyber
item.description: The US government warns programmable logic controllers are being targeted, and research turns up 179 vulnerable operational technology (OT) devices.
item.dc:creator: Robert Lemos

item.title: GlassWorm Campaign Uses Zig Dropper to Infect Multiple Developer IDEs
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have flagged yet another evolution of the&nbsp;ongoing GlassWorm campaign, which employs a new Zig dropper that's designed to stealthily infect all integrated development environments (IDEs) on a developer's&nbsp;machine. The technique has been discovered in an Open VSX extension named&nbsp;"specstudio.code-wakatime-activity-tracker," which masquerades as WakaTime, a
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Can Anthropic Keep Its Exploit-Writing AI Out of the Wrong Hands?
item.description: Its Mythos Preview model, which can allegedly find and exploit critical zero-days, also comes with certain controls, the vendor said.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Culafi

item.title: Just Three Ransomware Gangs Accounted for 40% of Attacks Last Month
item.description: Qilin, Akira and Dragonforce were responsible for 40% of 672 ransomware incidents reported in March, says Check Point

item.title: Google Chrome Rolls Out Protection Against Infostealers Targeting Session Cookies
item.description: Chrome’s Device Bound Session Credentials is designed to block infostealers from harvesting session cookie

item.title: Browser Extensions Are the New AI Consumption Channel That No One Is Talking About
item.description: While&nbsp;much of the discussion on AI security centers around protecting&nbsp;‘shadow’ AI and GenAI consumption, there's a wide-open&nbsp;window nobody's guarding: AI browser extensions.&nbsp; A&nbsp;new report from LayerX&nbsp;exposes just how deep this blind spot goes, and why AI extensions may be the most dangerous AI threat surface in your network&nbsp;that isn't&nbsp;on anyone's&nbsp;
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Sen. Sanders Talks to Claude About AI and Privacy
item.description: <p>Claude is actually <a href="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=h3AtWdeu_G0">pretty good</a> on the issues.</p>
item.dc:creator: Bruce Schneier

item.title: Recovery scammers hit you when you’re down: Here’s how to avoid a second strike
item.description: If you’ve been the victim of fraud, you’re likely already a lead on a ‘sucker list’ – and if you’re not careful, your ordeal may be about to get worse.

item.title: Google Rolls Out DBSC in Chrome 146 to Block Session Theft on Windows
item.description: Google has&nbsp;made Device Bound Session Credentials&nbsp;(DBSC) generally available to all Windows users of its Chrome web browser, months after&nbsp;it began&nbsp;testing the security feature in open&nbsp;beta. The public availability is currently limited to Windows users on Chrome 146, with macOS expansion planned in an upcoming Chrome&nbsp;release. "This project represents a significant
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Marimo RCE Flaw CVE-2026-39987 Exploited Within 10 Hours of Disclosure
item.description: A critical security vulnerability&nbsp;in Marimo, an open-source Python notebook for data science and analysis, has been exploited within 10 hours of public disclosure, according&nbsp;to findings from&nbsp;Sysdig. The vulnerability in question&nbsp;is CVE-2026-39987 (CVSS score: 9.3), a pre-authenticated remote code execution vulnerability impacting all versions of Marimo prior to and including
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: What is Shadow IT? | UpGuard
item.description: Explore more on what Shadow IT is, its examples, risks, and the rapid rise of Shadow AI. Learn how mid-market teams are bridging the visibility gap.

item.title: Backdoored Smart Slider 3 Pro Update Distributed via Compromised Nextend Servers
item.description: Unknown threat actors have hijacked the update system for the Smart Slider 3 Pro plugin for WordPress and Joomla to push a poisoned version containing a backdoor. The incident impacts Smart Slider 3 Pro version 3.5.1.35 for WordPress, per WordPress security company Patchstack. Smart Slider 3 is a popular WordPress slider plugin with more than 800,000 active installations across its free and Pro
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Russia's 'Fancy Bear' APT Continues Its Global Onslaught
item.description: Victims don't need to match the cyber espionage group's technical sophistication, experts say. But patching and some form of zero trust are now non-negotiable.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Culafi

item.title: 'BlueHammer' Windows Zero-Day Exploit Signals Microsoft Bug Disclosure Issues
item.description: Under the alias 'Chaotic Eclipse,' a researcher released a PoC exploit for a zero-day flaw that allows for system takeover by a local user, citing an undisclosed beef with Microsoft.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Montalbano

item.title: Hackers Exploit Adobe PDF Flaw for Months to Steal Data, No Fix Yet
item.description: <p>A critical Adobe Acrobat zero-day has been exploited for months via malicious PDFs to steal data and potentially take over systems, with no patch yet available.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-adobe-acrobat-zero-day-pdf-exploit-months/">Hackers Exploit Adobe PDF Flaw for Months to Steal Data, No Fix Yet</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Ken Underhill

item.title: Do Ceasefires Slow Cyberattacks? History Suggests Not
item.description: The cybersecurity community is waiting with bated breath to see if Iranian hackers will honor a ceasefire that doesn't actually name or directly involve them.
item.dc:creator: Nate Nelson

item.title: EngageLab SDK Flaw Exposed 50M Android Users, Including 30M Crypto Wallet Installs
item.description: Details have emerged about a now-patched security vulnerability in a widely used third-party Android software development kit&nbsp;(SDK) called&nbsp;EngageLab&nbsp;SDK that could have put millions of cryptocurrency wallet users at&nbsp;risk. "This flaw allows apps on the same device to bypass Android security sandbox and gain unauthorized access to private&nbsp;data," the Microsoft Defender
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: UAT-10362 Targets Taiwanese NGOs with LucidRook Malware in Spear-Phishing Campaigns
item.description: A previously undocumented threat cluster&nbsp;dubbed UAT-10362 has been attributed to spear-phishing campaigns targeting Taiwanese non-governmental organizations (NGOs) and suspected universities to deploy a new Lua-based malware called LucidRook. "LucidRook is a sophisticated stager that embeds a Lua interpreter and Rust-compiled libraries within a dynamic-link library (DLL) to download and
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: New Apple Scam Hits Millions of iPhone Users Worldwide, Draining Bank Accounts
item.description: <p>Apple warns of a new scam targeting millions of iPhone users. Learn the red flags, how it works, and how to protect your account and finances.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-apple-iphone-scam-targeting-millions-2026/">New Apple Scam Hits Millions of iPhone Users Worldwide, Draining Bank Accounts</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Aminu Abdullahi

item.title: Massive Data Breach Exposes 337K LAPD-Linked Records
item.description: <p>A massive breach exposed 337K LAPD-linked files, raising concerns over third-party risk, sensitive data exposure, and law enforcement cybersecurity gaps.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-lapd-data-breach-337k-files-exposed/">Massive Data Breach Exposes 337K LAPD-Linked Records</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Joseph Ofonagoro

item.title: STX RAT Targets Finance Sector With Advanced Stealth Tactics
item.description: STX RAT, a newly identified remote access trojan, attempted deployment in finance, showing advanced C2 and stealthy delivery methods

item.title: Bitcoin Depot Reports $3.6m Crypto Theft After System Breach
item.description: Bitcoin Depot has disclosed a cyber-attack that led to the theft of more than 50 Bitcoin, worth $3.66m, after hackers accessed its internal systems

item.title: ThreatsDay Bulletin: Hybrid P2P Botnet, 13-Year-Old Apache RCE and 18 More Stories
item.description: Thursday. Another week, another batch of things that probably should've been caught sooner but&nbsp;weren't. This&nbsp;one's got some range — old vulnerabilities getting new life, a few "why was that even possible" moments, attackers leaning on platforms and tools you'd normally trust without thinking twice. Quiet&nbsp;escalations more than loud zero-days, but the kind that matter more in
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: The Hidden Security Risks of Shadow AI in Enterprises
item.description: As&nbsp;AI tools become more accessible, employees are adopting them without formal approval from IT and security teams. While&nbsp;these tools may boost productivity, automate tasks, or fill gaps in existing workflows, they also operate outside the visibility of security teams, bypassing controls and creating new blind spots in what is known as shadow AI. While&nbsp;similar to the phenomenon of
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Atomic Stealer MacOS ClickFix Attack Bypasses Apple Security Warnings
item.description: macOS 26.4 update introduced security warnings into Terminal to prevent ClickFix attacks, so attackers have shifted to Script Editor instead

item.title: Adobe Reader Zero-Day Exploited via Malicious PDFs Since December 2025
item.description: Threat actors have been exploiting a previously unknown zero-day vulnerability in Adobe Reader using maliciously crafted PDF documents since at least December&nbsp;2025. The finding, detailed by EXPMON's Haifei Li, has&nbsp;been described as a highly-sophisticated PDF exploit.&nbsp;The artifact ("Invoice540.pdf") first appeared on the VirusTotal platform on November 28, 2025.&nbsp;A second&nbsp;
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: On Microsoft’s Lousy Cloud Security
item.dc:creator: Bruce Schneier

item.title: Middle East Hack-for-Hire Operation Traced to South Asian Cyber Espionage Group
item.description: A spear-phishing campaign which spread across the Middle East between 2023 and 2024 has now been linked to Bitter APT group

item.title: Bitter-Linked Hack-for-Hire Campaign Targets Journalists Across MENA Region
item.description: An&nbsp;apparent hack-for-hire campaign likely orchestrated by a threat actor with suspected ties to the Indian government targeted journalists, activists, and government officials across the Middle East and North Africa (MENA), according to findings&nbsp;from Access&nbsp;Now, Lookout,&nbsp;and SMEX. Two&nbsp;of the targets included prominent Egyptian journalists and government critics, Mostafa
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Governance Gaps Emerge as AI Agents Drive 76% Increase in NHIs
item.description: SANS Institute reveals that AI agents are behind a 76% surge in non-human identities

item.title: Google Warns of New Threat Group Targeting BPOs and Helpdesks
item.description: Google’s threat intel team warns UNC6783, a new extortion group possibly linked to the “Raccoon” persona, is targeting BPOs and enterprises

item.title: Higher Education Community Vendor Assessment Toolkit (Free Template) | UpGuard
item.description: Utilize this free questionnaire template to evaluate third-party vendors against the Higher Education Community Vendor Assessment Toolkit.

item.title: Lowering Cyber Insurance Premiums in the Education Industry | UpGuard
item.description: Find out why organizations in the education sector need to purchase cyber insurance and how they can lower cyber insurance premiums.

item.title: TPRM in Higher Education: Post-Implementation Best Practices | UpGuard
item.description: Explore post-implementation best practices in higher education—designed to protect student data, minimize vendor risk, and enhance your TPRM program.

item.title: 8 Steps to Cultivate a Culture of Risk Awareness in Higher Education | UpGuard
item.description: Discover the steps your higher education can take to cultivate a culture of risk awareness and fortify its defenses against attacks and data breaches.

item.title: Protecting Student Health Data: TPRM for Higher Education | UpGuard
item.description: Explore how higher education institutions can utilize TPRM strategies to secure sensitive student health data they collect and use with third parties.

item.title: What is HECVAT? Protecting Students from Vendor Security Risks | UpGuard
item.description: A comprehensive overview of HECVAT 4. Learn how to leverage it to reduce your third-party risk exposure and likelihood of student data compromise.

item.title: How to Comply with HECVAT in 2026 (Free Checklist) | UpGuard
item.description: If you're a vendor supporting the education sector, this checklist will help you prepare for any HECVAT questionnaires that come your way.

item.title: Russia's Forest Blizzard Nabs Rafts of Logins via SOHO Routers
item.description: Heard of fileless malware? How about malwareless cyber espionage? Russia's APT28 is spying on global organizations by modifying just one DNS setting in vulnerable routers.
item.dc:creator: Nate Nelson

item.title: Threat Actors Get Crafty With Emojis to Escape Detection
item.description: When 🤖 means &quot;bot available,&quot; 🧰 signifies &quot;toolkit,&quot; or 💰💰💰 translates to &quot;big ransom,&quot; bad actors can evade filters and keep it all on the down-low.
item.dc:creator: Jai Vijayan

item.title: AI-Led Remediation Crisis Prompts HackerOne to Pause Bug Bounties
item.description: Discovery used to be the bottleneck for open source bugs, but with automated discovery, remediation's the bottleneck, which bounties don't fund.
item.dc:creator: Jai Vijayan

item.title: New Chaos Variant Targets Misconfigured Cloud Deployments, Adds SOCKS Proxy
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have flagged a new variant&nbsp;ofmalware&nbsp;called Chaosthat'scapable of&nbsp;hitting misconfigured cloud deployments, marking an expansion of the botnet's targeting infrastructure. "Chaos malware is increasingly targeting misconfigured cloud deployments, expanding beyond its traditional focus on routers and edge devices,"&nbsp;Darktrace said in a new&nbsp;report.
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Why Operationalizing AI Security Is the Next Great Enterprise Hurdle
item.description: <p>NWN launches an AI-powered security platform to tackle tool sprawl, alert fatigue, and modern cyber threats in the era of agentic enterprises.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-nwn-ai-security-platform-tool-sprawl-alert-fatigue/">Why Operationalizing AI Security Is the Next Great Enterprise Hurdle</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Zeus Kerravala

item.title: 10 ChatGPT AI Prompts L1 SOC Analysts Can Use in Their Daily Work
item.description: <p>Discover 10 practical ChatGPT prompts SOC analysts can use to speed up triage, analyze threats, improve documentation, and enhance incident response workflows.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-chatgpt-prompts-soc-analysts-incident-response/">10 ChatGPT AI Prompts L1 SOC Analysts Can Use in Their Daily Work</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Ken Underhill
